title: التنمر الإلكتروني: أذى متكرر يتضاعف بالنشر المجهول

As for young people, since definitions, both involve a real or perceived power imbalance that’s physical, they spend a lot of time in social media, psychological and/or social. Although cyberbullying occurs in digital spaces and can their interaction – good, bad or neutral – be anonymous, there’s usually a connection to offline life – for kids, school life. happens in apps, texts, games and sites too. Anonymity is more of a factor in cyberbullying than in traditional bullying. Targets may DEFINING CYBERBULLYING believe that more people are witness to the abuse than actually are, which can compound the pain. And since online socializing can occur 24/7, home, weekends and vacation can’t be havens from the hurt. Even though it has been around for ages, experts still haven’t completely agreed on a definition of “bullying,” much less YOUNG PEOPLE MAY USE DIFFERENT TERMS cyberbullying, the digital version. There are elements that keep popping up in definitions, though, so that we’re pretty If you want to talk with kids about “cyberbullying,” it might be best not to use that term clear on what it is not. text_simplified: التنمر الإلكتروني عادةً موجَّه ومتكرر، وقد يكون أقل مباشرةً من التنمر وجهاً لوجه لكنه مؤذٍ بالقدر نفسه عندما يُنشر على نطاق واسع أو من حسابات مجهولة. وهو يطال حياة الصغار أينما كانوا — في المدرسة وفي الألعاب والتطبيقات اليومية.
